note: fences_presets is a submodule of fences. It adds a `fences_preset` config entity bundling the eight Fences wrapper values under a name, plus a non-persisted 'Select Preset' dropdown injected into each field's Fences settings via hook_fences_field_formatter_third_party_settings_form_alter. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded documented facts); no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal site using the Fences module, I keep setting the same wrapper tags on many fields. Which Fences submodule lets me save a reusable named bundle of those tag settings, what entity type does it add, and where do I manage them?
evals.expected_output: Enable the fences_presets submodule (part of the fences project). It adds a `fences_preset` config entity; manage them at Configuration > User interface > Fences > Presets (route entity.fences_preset.collection), gated by the `administer fences_preset` permission.

evals.prompt: Which presets does the Fences Presets submodule ship out of the box, and what does the 'Inline' preset do to a field's markup?
evals.expected_output: It ships four default presets: default, inline, inline_label, and none. The 'inline' preset sets the field tag to `span` and the item, label, and items-wrapper tags to `none`, so multiple inline fields render next to each other with no block wrappers.

evals.prompt: When I pick a preset from the 'Select Preset' dropdown on a field's Fences settings, is the field permanently linked to that preset? How does applying a preset actually work in fences_presets?
evals.expected_output: No — the preset selector is not persisted (it is rendered via an inline_template specifically so its value is not saved). Selecting a preset just runs JavaScript that copies the preset's tag values into the field's real Fences settings controls, which you then save normally. It is a one-shot template, not a live link, so editing the preset later does not change fields that used it. The selector and preset data are injected via hook_fences_field_formatter_third_party_settings_form_alter.
